commit.message:
Remove remember-password functionality in favor of Keychain

The password-file-based "remember password" feature stored plaintext
passwords on disk. With macOS Keychain auto-unlock now integrated,
this is redundant and less secure. Remove all password-file storage,
the remember toggle from all UI screens, and the /remember API
endpoints.

commit.message:
Point SARA+ button to CustomerRecords page directly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: preserve sparse arrays through the reactive pipeline (#2952)

* feat: preserve sparse arrays through the reactive pipeline

Fixes six sites across four source files that destroyed array sparseness
(holes) by using spread, .map(), or for...of + push. Uses forEach for
sparse iteration where possible (structural hole-skipping), and for +
`i in` where absence detection or early exit is needed (diff engine,
isStorableArray). Also fixes traverseArrayWithSchema and its caller.

Rewrites the design doc as a reference for new engineers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: densify sparse arrays at merkle-reference hashing boundary

The merkle-reference library can't hash sparse array holes (throws
TypeError: Unknown type undefined). Densify by converting holes to null
in wrappedNodeBuilder.toTree before passing to the default node builder.
The canonical hash path already handles holes natively.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add end-to-end sparse array map test

Verifies that map() preserves sparse array holes: input [10, <hole>, 30]
produces output [20, <hole>, 60] with only two pattern runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update design doc for identity-based map and hashing fix

- Map builtin section now describes the #2942 identity-based reconciliation
  loop instead of the old initializedUpTo loop
- Added hashing boundary section for reference.ts workaround
- Removed stale "merkle hashing prevents e2e tests" limitation
- Added patterns-core.test.ts to test coverage list

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

- 1000 AccessControlCardRec records (unlock/access log)
- Device uses object-based RecordFinder API (factory.create -> object ID)

  doFind -> stopFind -> destroy flow

- get_call_logs: new method using VideoTalkLog

commit.message:
⚡ Bolt: Optimize duplicate detection DB writes in scan flow (#203)

* ⚡ Bolt: Optimize DB writes in contact scan

Moves duplicate mapping logic to happen completely in-memory before the main database insert (`contactDao.replaceAllContacts`). This avoids an expensive secondary pass of `getContactsByIds` and subsequent `insertContacts` queries for every contact flagged as a duplicate.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit findings in ContactRepositoryImpl

- Fix stale data on zero contacts: clear DB and update summary when
  device returns 0 contacts instead of returning empty result
- Fix incorrect scanned count: use actual finalEntities.size instead
  of fallback totalToProcess (1000) for usage tracking
- Map matchingKey in toDomain() to prevent silent data loss

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: code fence stripping, iMessage gateway stuck, DB path validation

commands.rs: trim_start_matches("```") then trim_start_matches(language)
treated the language name as a character set, stripping individual chars
from code completions (e.g. language "ts" would eat leading t/s chars).
Replaced with strip_prefix for exact literal matching at both call sites.

gateway.rs: iMessage poll() skipped last_rowid update on empty-text
messages (Tapback reactions), causing the gateway to permanently loop
on the same ROWID and never process subsequent messages.

commands.rs: Replaced naive DB path validation (string contains ".." +
wrong operator precedence) with validate_sqlite_path() that uses
canonicalize to resolve symlinks and blocks system dirs + home dotfiles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
test: add unit tests for occurrence-generator and permissions-service

occurrence-generator (20 tests):
- validateRRule: accepts DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/COUNT/UNTIL/INTERVAL rules,
  rejects invalid strings, rejects empty string with error message
- previewOccurrences: empty string → [], returns Date instances, respects
  count limit, exact count for unlimited DAILY, bounded by COUNT, weekly
  7-day gaps, BYDAY=MO generates Mondays only, invalid rule → [] (swallowed),
  null dtstart handled, default count=10, all dates >= dtstart

permissions-service (16 tests):
- hasPermission: admin (role 0) bypasses all checks, admin bypass with empty
  perms, user with permission returns true, missing perm returns false, empty
  perms list, case-sensitive match, admin with multiple roles, exact string match
- hasRole: user with matching role, single element match, no matching role,
  empty allowed list, empty user roles, both empty, OR logic across roles,
  admin role 0 not special (pure membership check)

commit.message:
feat: auto-recover from migration ordering mismatch (#427) (#433)

Add opt-in `auto_reset_on_migration_failure` config flag (default false).
When enabled and migrations fail due to version mismatch, the API drops
and recreates the database then retries migrations once. Designed for
the ephemeral demo environment where the DB is re-seeded every 30 min.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Hansen-Hodrick predictive regression tests for VIX-gating signal

Implements formal statistical tests for whether VIX term structure
inversion predicts spread variance collapse -- the theoretical
mechanism behind HAMMER's rebalance blocking.

Features:
- 6 regression specifications (continuous/binary signal, RV/spread, controls)
- Hansen-Hodrick (1980) SEs with uniform kernel for overlapping horizons
- Newey-West (Bartlett kernel) SEs for robustness comparison
- SE inflation ratios showing how much OLS understates uncertainty
- Scatter plots, SE comparison charts, time series overlays

Key results (GC HAMMER Max Growth, h=21d):
- VIX slope -> spread variance: beta=-0.000365, p=0.026 (HH) SIGNIFICANT
- SE inflation: 10-13x vs naive OLS
- Signal predictive power attenuates with controls (lagged RV)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v2.9.1: Fix 17 bugs found in self-audit (5 critical, 5 high, 7 medium)

Critical fixes:
- Rewrite Supabase Realtime: correct postgres_changes protocol (v2)
- Add wss://*.supabase.co to CSP connect-src (was silently blocked)
- Fix heartbeat interval leak on WebSocket reconnect
- Fix folioCounters in _deepMergeDB: always use Math.max (prevents folio collision)
- Fix nextFolio: markDirty() immediately after increment

High fixes:
- Photo total size budget: warn at 2.5MB, block at 3.5MB (prevent localStorage quota crash)
- Fix autolock PIN dots hidden permanently after wrong attempt (display:none removed)
- Client ID uses randomized timestamp (prevents cross-device collision)
- Photo data URI validated before img src injection in certificates
- Autolock rate limit persisted to localStorage (survives page reload)

Medium fixes:
- _deepMergeDB no longer drops items without IDs
- _passHash and _salts protected from remote overwrite (prevents lockout)
- Vitrina repairs now follow apartado payment rules
- setPendingHandoff awaits saveData()
- Exponential backoff on WebSocket reconnect (5s → 5min cap)
- Supabase Realtime handles both postgres_changes and legacy event formats

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add v1 notifications API with paginated list and type filtering (#712)

Expose /api/v1/notifications endpoints for mobile consumption:
- GET /api/v1/notifications — paginated list with offset/limit, type filter
- GET /api/v1/notifications/{id} — single notification detail
- POST /api/v1/notifications/{id}/read — mark as read
- POST /api/v1/notifications/read-all — mark all as read
- GET /api/v1/notifications/unread-count — unread count (replaces old route)

Includes NotificationResource with simplified type categories
(transaction, security, system, promo) and 10 feature tests.

commit.message:
Document correct source3 decomposition: bound d/2 - b, not c - d/2

The naive decomposition source3 ≤ ε·m + max(0, c - d/2) is false when
strangers above D's range push C-native items to lower ranks. The correct
bound is source3 ≤ ε·m + max(0, d/2 - b), where b = B-native count.

Since d/2 - b = (c - d/2) + s, bounding the B-native deficit automatically
accounts for both C-native excess AND stranger displacement. The benchmark
comparison (Seiferas Section 5) bounds this total quantity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: marker click handlers for Google Maps API gmp-click migration

Google Maps deprecated addListener('click') on AdvancedMarkerElement.
Physical clicks on custom content elements no longer fire via the old
API. Switched to addEventListener('gmp-click') plus direct click
listeners on content elements. Also lazy-creates InfoWindow in the
click handler to survive Vite HMR reloads.

commit.message:
fix: prevent auto-sync from regressing ProphetX status backward

When a real-world source is behind ProphetX (e.g. SDIO still shows
Scheduled while ProphetX correctly reports live), the mismatch detector
was overwriting ProphetX's correct status with the stale source value.

Added a lifecycle guard (not_started → live → ended) that refuses to
move status backward for system-initiated syncs. Manual/admin overrides
are still allowed.

commit.message:
Allow null values in named parameter maps (#17) (#17)

Remove filterNotNull() from map-based query/execute overloads that
was stripping null parameter values and causing positional parameter
count mismatches in bindParameters. Widen List<Any> to List<Any?> in
the positional overloads so that overload resolution works correctly
when resolveParams returns List<Any?>. Also apply same fix to suspend
variants.
